q: The reaction where reactants are converted into products is ________ reaction.
Heterogenous chemical equilibrium, the reactants and the products of chemical equilibrium are all in ________.
Equilibrium in which all substances are in the same phases are called ________.
What does the dotted curve in the diagram given below represent?

Methane reacts with steam and an equilibrium is reached.
$$\text{CH}_4\text{(g)} + \text{H}_2\text{O(l)} \rightleftharpoons \text{CO(g)} + 3\text{H}_2\text{(g)} \quad \Delta\text{H} = (+)$$
Which of the following changes the increasing amount of hydrogen?

A reaction between nitrogen and oxygen is shown. The forward reaction is endothermic.
$$\text{N}_2\text{(g)} + \text{O}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NO(g)}$$
Which change increases the equilibrium yield of nitrogen monoxide, NO?
The equilibrium $\text{N}_2\text{(g)} + \text{O}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NO(g)}$, is an example of ________ chemical equilibrium.
In which of the following conditions do you think the rates of both forward and backward reactions are the same?
$\text{H}_2\text{O(l)} \rightleftharpoons \text{H}_2\text{O(g)}$ is in ________ chemical equilibrium.
At equilibrium the rate of the forward reaction is ________ the rate of reverse reaction.

An equilibrium reaction is shown.
$$\text{H}_2\text{(g)} + \text{I}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{HI(g)}$$
What is the effect of increasing pressure on the equilibrium position?
After a system has reached equilibrium, as shown by the dotted line, the rate of forward and the reverse reaction are equal rate. Which of the dotted-lined show?

An equilibrium reaction is shown.
$$\text{N}_2\text{O}_4\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NO}_2\text{(g)} \quad \Delta\text{H} = (+)$$
What is the effect of increasing temperature on the equilibrium position?
At dynamic equilibrium the concentration of both the reactants and products are ________
The equilibrium in a chemical reaction is represented by the symbol ________

For the endothermic reaction; $\text{CaCO}_3\text{(s)} \rightleftharpoons \text{CaO(s)} + \text{CO}_2\text{(g)}$
Le Chatelier's principle predicts that ________ will result in an increase in the amount of $\text{CO}_2$.
The reaction proceeding to the left is called the ________ reaction.

The reaction used to manufacture ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen is reversible.
An equilibrium is established between ammonia, nitrogen and hydrogen.
Which statement describes the equilibrium?
a: Both the forward reaction and the backward reaction have the same rate.
The rate of the backward reaction is greater than the rate of the forward reaction.
The rate of the forward reaction is greater than the rate of the backward reaction.
The forward and backward reactions have both stopped

q: A book at rest on a table is an example of ________ equilibrium.
The effect of a catalyst on an equilibrium is to ________.
a: shift the equilibrium to the right
increase the rate of the forward reaction only
increase the rate at which equilibrium is achieved without changing the composition of the equilibrium mixture

q: What does the straight curve in the diagram given above represent?
Homogeneous chemical chemical equilibrium, the reactants and the products of chemical equilibrium are all in ________.
Equilibrium is said to be dynamic because the ________.

Consider the following reaction at equilibrium:
$$\text{CO(g)} + 2\text{H}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons \text{CH}_3\text{OH(g)} \quad \Delta\text{H} = (-)$$
Le Chatelier's principle predicts that an increase in pressure will result in ________.
a: more yield of CO(g) and H2(g)

q:
The equation shows the equilibrium between dinitrogen tetroxide, $\text{N}_2\text{O}_4$, and nitrogen dioxide, $\text{NO}_2$. The colours of the reactant and product are also shown.
$$\text{N}_2\text{O}_4\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NO}_2\text{(g)} \quad \Delta\text{H} = (+)$$
colourless, reddish-brown
Which statement is not correct?
a: At equilibrium the concentration of the reactant and the product are constant.
At equilibrium, the rate of the forward reaction is equal to the rate of the reverse reaction.
When the pressure is increased, the reaction mixture becomes reddish-brown.
When the temperature increased, the reaction mixture becomes reddish-brown.

q: Equilibrium in which two or more phases are present in the reaction are called ________.
Which of a following reaction is reversible?
a: fuels are burned in the air
decomposition of calcium carbonate in open vessel
synthesis of ammonia by Haber process

q: Which statement about a reaction in equilibrium is not correct?
a: Both the forward and the backward reactions are proceeding at the same rate.
There is no further change in the net concentration.
The reactions continuous to take place.
The amount of product present is only affected by a change in pressure.

q: If both the forward and reverse reaction rates are equal in the equilibrium is said to be ________.
In which of the following reactions would increasing pressure at constant temperature not change the concentrations of reactants and products, based on Le Chatelier's principle?

Consider the following reaction at equilibrium.
$$\text{CaO(s)} + \text{CO}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons \text{CaCO}_3\text{(s)} \quad \Delta\text{H} = (-)$$
Le Chatelier's principle predicts that the equilibrium to get the highest quantity of $\text{CaCO}_3\text{(s)}$ on the right-hand side by carrying out the reaction ________.
a: at increasing temperature and increasing pressure
at increasing temperature and decreasing pressure
at decreasing temperature and increasing pressure
at decreasing temperature and decreasing pressure

q:
From the given three statements, which one is NOT true about the following reaction?
$$\text{N}_2\text{(g)} + 3\text{H}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NH}_3\text{(g)}$$
a: It is a heterogenous equilibrium reaction.
It is a homogenous equilibrium reaction.

q: Inside the sealed soda bottle, the two forms of carbon dioxide are in equilibrium.
The reactions in which products can turn back into reactants are ________ reaction.
